title: Lennar posts 2Q earnings beat as demand for affordable housing outstrips supply
text: Lennar (NYSE:LEN) has reported second-quarter revenue and earnings that beat expectations as the homebuilder benefited from a market where demand is outstripping supply. The Miami-based company noted that while its average sales price per home delivered was $449,000 in the second quarter, compared to almost $500,000 at the peak last year, home deliveries reached 17,074, up 3% and new orders were 17,885, up 1%, year over year.
